summary: Set 073 Session 2 of 2 -- synthesis + (conditional) template fix + close. Ran on claude-opus-4-8 (operator started this session; the S1 routed recommendation suggested sonnet/normal for cost, operator override). An APPLICATION/TELEMETRY + SYNTHESIS session: NO production code changed (ai_router untouched), NO version bump, NO PyPI release, NO extension/Marketplace change. DELIVERED: (1) SYNTHESIS -- authored docs/verification-surface-strategy.md SECTION 9 (the second cross-target datapoint): the Gemini-pull replication verdict recorded HONESTLY as 'non-silent: REPLICATED (N=2)' (clean VERIFIED on both harvester and platform under strong adversarial framing) WITH the honest nuance that the finding-YIELD gap is NOT refuted (Gemini-pull 0 findings vs GPT-pull 2 Major contract-drift over the same platform repo) -- replication is of the verdict-not-silence property, not finding power; the push-blindness contrast across targets (golden-dominated harvester diff = push non-load-bearing vs source-dominated platform diff = push a genuine reviewing surface, both elided at ~61KB); the cross-target aggregator exercised on real independently-produced inputs + the MixedTargetError contrapositive; the consumer-handoff value (2 real repo-state findings dabbler-platform can remediate directly). The live router-config.yaml default pull provider is recorded as STILL HELD (sec 5.1 / 8.3 / 9.1) -- N=2 on one property does not move it, and the finding-yield read actually cautions AGAINST promoting Gemini to the default pull slot on this evidence. (2) CONDITIONAL TEMPLATE FIX = NO-OP (recorded, sec 9.3): the recurrence gate did not trip -- the Set 072 pull-template instruction-tension meta-finding did NOT recur on platform (GPT-pull emitted substantive contract-drift findings instead), so it is a single observation (N=1). Per spec item 5: record the single observation, ship NO template change and NO release. classify_framing_strength stays ADVERSARIAL (templates untouched). pull-verifier.md deliberately NOT updated -- its per-set sections are as-built-CODE records and no code shipped. (3) change-log.md authored (both sessions; no release). (4) Lesson L-073-1 authored -- 'a replication confirms one property, not a headline; name which held and which did not' (cites L-064-8 stay-within-evidence kin, L-065-1 echo discipline). Guidance freshness headers refreshed (lessons-learned.md now 93% of ceiling -- a pruning sweep will be required before adding much more; operator-reviewed archival, not automatic). VERIFICATION: routed_gate REQUIRED (blast-radius cross-artifact + breadth 8 files); cross-provider session-verification by openai/gpt-5.4 (cross-provider from the anthropic orchestrator) returned VERIFIED, 0 blocking findings, 1 NIT (cross-target-comparison.md forward-referenced the synthesis as 'sec 8'; it landed in the new sec 9). FIXED the 2 forward refs to sec 9 (left the correct sec 8.3/8.4 refs to Set 072 content) per L-065-1; NOT re-verified (a confirming re-verify of a pure wording fix is the L-071-1 nit-churn the discipline forbids). s2-verification.md saved raw. ADVISORY DOGFOOD (pathAwareCritique: advisory): multi-provider path-aware critique (openai gpt-5.4 + google gemini-2.5-pro) over the set diff -> path-aware-critique.json (ok=True). Gemini VERIFIED 0 findings; GPT-5.4 1 Major contract-drift (change-log.md says CLOSED/VERIFIED while session-state.json still in-progress). ADJUDICATED false-positive -- a pre-close TIMING artifact (L-070-1): the change-log close-out language is the standard forward-looking convention every set writes just before close_session, the critic ran BEFORE close-out, and its own concrete fix ('run close_session') is the imminent next step. Drives NO new doc/code change, so the dogfood converges; the authoritative verdict is the cross-provider session verification (VERIFIED). Session 2 VERIFIED. With S2 complete the set CLOSES (S1 + S2 both VERIFIED).

reason.specifics: Set 073 is COMPLETE (both sessions VERIFIED; no release). The routed next-SESSION-SET recommendation (route(task_type=analysis) -> google/gemini-pro, cross-provider from the claude orchestrator per L-064-6; saved raw to s2-next-session.md) is: PRIMARY = Set 074, the GREENFIELD finding-POWER track -- run the provider x surface matrix during a greenfield build to test raw CATCH POWER (catch rate), the one property the already-built-target runs (harvester, platform) structurally CANNOT measure and the property N=2 did NOT establish (they test cost/noise/false-positive rate). Rejected alternatives: a THIRD external target (diminishing returns -- N=2 on non-silence is enough and it would not close the finding-power gap); the DEFAULT-PULL-PROVIDER decision set (premature -- the finding-yield gap cautions against it now); the RETIRE/powered-equal-arms benchmark track (still blocked on consumer-repo field-pilot data). The NEXT action is SPEC AUTHORING for Set 074, which per the operator's standing preference is AUDIT-then-spec for substantial features: audit/scope the greenfield-build target + the matrix-during-build seam before authoring. claude-sonnet-4-6 @ normal (tier 2) is the cheapest-capable choice for spec authoring/scoping; the operator may override at the next set's start. Operator note: this is a separate, larger track than the cheap cross-target replication -- confirm scope (which greenfield build, how the matrix attaches) before committing.
